secure.permanenceHeadline: There's no way to recover this key if you lose it.
secure.permanenceBody: No reset, no support line, no email recovery. Save it before continuing.

csae.title: Child Safety Policy

donorGuide.seoDescription: How donating works on {{appName}} and how to protect your privacy.
donorGuide.title: Donor Guide
donorGuide.subtitle: Real Bitcoin, sent directly. The whole flow in one short page.

recipientGuide.seoDescription: How to receive donations on {{appName}} and cash out privately.
recipientGuide.title: Recipient Guide
recipientGuide.subtitle: Pick what to accept, receive donations, cash out.

changelog.title: Changelog
changelog.preReleaseBody: This build contains changes not yet included in a release.

about.seoDescription: How {{appName}} works: connecting recipients to unstoppable funding through Bitcoin and Nostr.

hero.body: {{appName}} is a censorship-resistant donation platform built on Nostr and Bitcoin. No frozen bank accounts. No corporate shut-downs. Just direct support from people who believe in your cause.

sampleCard.imageAlt: Venezuelans at a candlelight vigil holding a sign reading 'Liberen a todos los presos políticos · ¡Cese la represión!'
sampleCard.title: Free Venezuela's Political Prisoners
sampleCard.description: Legal defense and family support for 800+ political prisoners detained by the Maduro regime.

howItWorks.title: Three steps. No middleman.
howItWorks.lede: No banks, no borders, no permission.

step1.imageAlt: A glowing orange cryptographic key floating in dark space, symbolizing self-sovereign identity
step1.title: Recipients sign up in seconds.
step1.body: No bank. No paperwork. No approval. The moment a recipient creates an account on {{appName}}, they have a Bitcoin address ready to receive support, anywhere in the world.

step2.imageAlt: A glowing Bitcoin coin flying through the air across a dark world map, symbolizing instant cross-border payment
step2.title: Donors send Bitcoin directly.
step2.body: Donors send Bitcoin from any wallet they already use (Cash App, Coinbase, Strike, a hardware wallet) straight to the recipient. {{appName}} never touches the money. No server in the middle, no custodian.

step3.imageAlt: An open hand with warm orange light radiating from the palm, symbolizing receiving and agency
step3.title: Support lands where it matters.
step3.body: Funds arrive at the recipient directly. They keep custody, they keep control. Move it, swap it, or spend it; the guides below explain how to do it privately and safely.

twoWays.title: Two ways to get paid.
twoWays.lede: Both options are Bitcoin. The difference is what you trade off for privacy. When a recipient creates a campaign, they choose which options to accept. If both, the donation page shows a single QR code that works for any wallet.

public.title: Works with every wallet on earth.
public.description: Donations land at a regular Bitcoin address the recipient controls. Anyone with any Bitcoin wallet can send. No new app, no new account, no learning curve.
public.bullet1: Works in every Bitcoin wallet: Cash App, Coinbase, Strike, hardware
public.bullet3: Verifiable on-chain: anyone can see what a recipient has received
public.tradeoffIntro: Every donation is public on the Bitcoin blockchain and on Nostr. {{appName}} is recommended only for above-ground campaigns. If you or your donors require extreme privacy, including from state actors, read the <0>Donor Guide</0> and <1>Recipient Guide</1> before participating.

silent.title: Unlinkable, on-chain, direct.
silent.description: Donations are sent as BIP-352 silent payments. Each one lands at a fresh, unlinkable Bitcoin output that an observer staring at the blockchain can't tie back to the campaign.
silent.bullet1: Donation trail can't be reconstructed on-chain
silent.bullet3: Kept in a separate private balance, never mixed with public funds
silent.tradeoffIntro: The most private way to receive Bitcoin on-chain, but the ecosystem is young and rough today:

noCustody.title: No custody. No middleman.
noCustody.body: {{appName}} never holds funds. Donations move wallet-to-wallet on Bitcoin. There's no server standing between donor and recipient on either option. If {{appName}} disappeared tomorrow, every campaign would keep working.

gofundme.heading: Unlike GoFundMe
gofundme.body: No platform can freeze your donations, demand refunds, or terminate your campaign over policy disagreements.

givesendgo.heading: Unlike GiveSendGo
givesendgo.body: No payment processor sits in the middle, so no Stripe, no Visa, no bank can cut you off mid-campaign.

otherBitcoin.heading: Unlike other ‘Bitcoin’ platforms
otherBitcoin.body: No central Lightning node, custodian, or LSP to fail or go offline. Funds settle directly on-chain to a wallet you control.

chapter1.label: Getting started
chapter1.description: What Agora is, who built it, and what it costs.

chapter2.label: Bitcoin donations
chapter2.description: How payments work, why on-chain, why public, why these trade-offs.

chapter3.label: About Nostr
chapter3.description: The open protocol Agora is built on, and how your account works.

guides.title: Pick the side you're on.
guides.lede: Whether you're sending or receiving, learn how to do it safely and privately.

donor.imageAlt: Hooded protesters marching through a European city street at dusk, holding burning red flares and 'FREEDOM' and 'LIBERTAD' banners
donor.title: Support causes the banks won't.
donor.description: Send Bitcoin directly to recipients and movements anywhere in the world, without asking a payment processor for permission.
donor.bullet1: Use any Bitcoin wallet you already have.
donor.bullet2: Donations land directly with the recipient. No custodian, no middleman.
donor.bullet3: For privacy, use a wallet that supports silent payments.

recipient.imageAlt: Aerial view of thousands of protesters gathered at night in a city square with burning red flares, beneath a 'UNITY IN DARKNESS' banner projected on the surrounding buildings
recipient.title: Get funded without permission.
recipient.description: Receive support directly from people who believe in your cause. No bank account, no application form, no company in the middle.
recipient.bullet1: Start receiving donations as soon as you sign up.
recipient.bullet2: Pick which payment types to accept: public, silent payments, or both.
recipient.bullet3: For private cash-out, send to a silent-payments wallet first, then forward anywhere.

corporateSponsorship.seoDescription: Partner with {{appName}} to fund causes that matter. Donate to the Seed Fund, match donations to campaigns you care about, or promote {{appName}} to your customers as part of your philanthropic initiatives.

hero.body: {{appName}} connects donors directly to causes through Bitcoin — no frozen accounts, no middlemen, no platform taking a cut. Bring the same uncensorable, transparent giving to your company's philanthropy.

intro.title: Corporate giving without the gatekeepers
intro.lede: Every dollar your company commits goes straight to the cause — verifiably, on the Bitcoin blockchain. There is no custodian to freeze funds, no payment processor to deplatform a campaign, and no opaque overhead eating into your impact.

stats.reachLabel: Borders that can stop a donation

ways.title: Three ways to partner
ways.lede: Pick the level of involvement that fits your company — or combine all three.

seedFund.title: Donate to the Agora Seed Fund
seedFund.description: Make an unrestricted gift to the {{appName}} Seed Fund. The Seed Fund seeds new and high-need campaigns across the platform, getting verified causes off the ground when they need it most.
seedFund.bullet1: Give in Bitcoin (BTC) or US dollars (USD)
seedFund.bullet2: Unrestricted — directed where the need is greatest

matching.title: Match donations to campaigns
matching.description: Commit a matching pool and we will double every donor's gift up to your cap. Choose exactly what your company stands behind:
matching.bullet1: Match an individual campaign you care about
matching.bullet2: Match a featured or staff-picked campaign
matching.bullet3: Match every campaign on a curated list — e.g. campaigns supporting political prisoners, or women's sovereignty across Africa

promote.title: Promote {{appName}} to your customers
promote.description: Make {{appName}} giving a centerpiece of your philanthropic initiatives. Invite your customers and community to give alongside you, and turn your reach into real, uncensorable funding for causes.
promote.bullet2: Checkout round-ups and customer match prompts
promote.bullet3: Shared transparency reports your customers can verify on-chain

matchingExamples.title: Match the causes that move you
matchingExamples.lede: Curated lists let you back an entire movement with a single commitment. A few examples of what a matching pool can target:

example1.title: Political prisoners
example1.body: Match every donation to campaigns providing legal defense and family support for political prisoners worldwide.

example2.title: Women's sovereignty in Africa
example2.body: Double gifts to campaigns advancing women's economic independence and self-determination across the African continent.

example3.title: Your own list
example3.body: Tell us the cause and we'll build a curated list of verified campaigns for your matching pool to back.

cta.title: Ready to put your company behind a cause?
cta.body: Tell us how you'd like to get involved and we'll help you set up the right partnership — Seed Fund gift, matching pool, or a customer campaign.

campaignsCreate.title: Title
campaignsCreate.walletHeroNote:
Donations flow straight into your own Agora wallet.
No middleman.
campaignsCreate.walletHeroReassurance: You hold the key, so you hold the funds. Withdraw any time from the wallet tab.
campaignsCreate.acceptAllHint: Take both public and private donations.
campaignsCreate.acceptPublicHint: Donors give to a regular Bitcoin address. These donations are visible to anyone.
campaignsCreate.acceptPrivateHint: Donors give privately, so their identity stays hidden from the public.
campaignsCreate.customWalletIntro: Fill in whichever donations you want to accept: a public address, a private code, or both. At least one is required.
campaignsCreate.customOnchainMeaning: Public. Anyone can see these donations.
campaignsCreate.customSpMeaning: Private. The donor's identity stays hidden.
campaignsCreate.onchainInvalid: Not a recognized mainnet Bitcoin address (bc1q… / bc1p…).
campaignsCreate.spInvalid: Not a recognized BIP-352 silent-payment code (sp1…).
campaignsCreate.storyPlaceholder: Share the background, who benefits, and how funds will be used.
campaignsCreate.goalNote: Whole US Dollars. Donors pay in Bitcoin; clients estimate the USD-equivalent at view time.
campaignsCreate.errorLoginRequired: You must be logged in to create a campaign.
campaignsCreate.errorEditLoadFailed: Campaign could not be loaded for editing.
campaignsCreate.errorEditNotOwner: Only the campaign author can edit this campaign.
campaignsCreate.errorTitleInvalid: Title must include letters or numbers so a campaign URL can be created.
campaignsCreate.errorSlugInvalid: Identifier must be lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens.
campaignsCreate.errorHdUnavailable: Built-in wallet is unavailable for this login.
campaignsCreate.errorSpUnavailable: Silent-payment address is unavailable for this login.
campaignsCreate.errorOnchainInvalid: The on-chain address is not a recognized mainnet bech32(m) address (bc1q… / bc1p…).
campaignsCreate.errorSpInvalid: The silent-payment code is not a recognized BIP-352 code (sp1…).
campaignsCreate.errorWalletRequired: Provide at least one wallet endpoint — a Bitcoin mainnet address (bc1q… / bc1p…) or a silent-payment code (sp1…).
campaignsCreate.errorGoalInvalid: Goal must be a positive whole-dollar amount.

campaignsCreate.errorBannerInvalid: Banner must be a valid https:// URL.
campaignsCreate.errorHdDeriveFailed: Could not derive a fresh on-chain address from your wallet.
campaignsCreate.errorHdDeriveInvalid: Derived wallet address failed validation. Please add a custom address instead.
campaignsCreate.errorPublishedInvalid: Published event failed validation. Please refresh and try again.

campaignVerification.attestation: By verifying this campaign you attest that the campaign is authentic and true. You know the campaign organizer personally or have verified meaningfully that their claims are true. Beware of impersonators and scammers.

home.seoDescription: Connecting the world to unstoppable funding.
home.heroBody: Raise Bitcoin directly from supporters around the world. Every donation settles straight to your wallet, with no middlemen, no chargebacks, and no platform holding your funds.
home.allCampaignsDesc: Every campaign on the network, in chronological order.
home.noMatchHint: Try a different search term, or clear the search.
home.hiddenDesc: Campaigns suppressed from public discovery. Use the kebab menu on a card to unhide.
home.yourCampaignsDesc: Your campaigns are live on Nostr and donations work via the campaign link. Browse all campaigns at /campaigns; the {{appName}} team features a curated selection on the homepage.
home.emptyHint: Be the first to start a fundraiser on {{appName}}. Tell your story, choose your beneficiaries, and share the link.

venezuelaRelief.title: Venezuela needs <0>you</0>
venezuelaRelief.body: A powerful earthquake has struck northern Venezuela, hitting Caracas and La Guaira hardest. Families there can't get through this alone. Your donation goes straight to relief campaigns on the ground, turning your compassion into shelter, food, and hope today.
venezuelaRelief.credit: Photos from Caracas, Venezuela. (AP; AFP via Getty Images)
venezuelaRelief.popupBody: A powerful earthquake has struck northern Venezuela, hitting Caracas and La Guaira hardest. Your donation goes straight to relief campaigns on the ground, turning compassion into shelter, food, and hope today.
venezuelaRelief.seoDescription: A powerful earthquake has struck northern Venezuela. Donate Bitcoin directly to on-the-ground relief campaigns on Agora. No platform, no custodian, no permission required.
venezuelaRelief.pageHowBody: Agora is non-custodial: your Bitcoin goes straight from your wallet to a relief campaign's address. No platform takes a cut, no custodian holds the funds, and no one needs permission to give or receive. Every campaign below is run by people responding to the quake on the ground.

whyDifferent.title: Built different.
whyDifferent.lede: Direct Bitcoin from donor to recipient. No platform in the way, no custodian holding the bag, no permission required.

block1.heading: Unlike GoFundMe
block1.body: No platform can freeze your donations, demand refunds, or terminate your campaign over policy disagreements. No Stripe, no Visa, no bank sits in the middle and can cut you off mid-campaign.
block1.bullet2: No payment processor can pull the plug

block2.heading: Unlike other ‘Bitcoin’ platforms
block2.body: No central Lightning node, custodian, or LSP to fail or go offline. Funds settle directly on Bitcoin to a wallet you control. If {{appName}} disappeared tomorrow, every campaign would keep working.
block2.bullet1: No custodial wallet to drain or freeze
block2.bullet2: Settles on-chain to a wallet you own

block3.heading: Public or private. Your choice.
block3.body: Recipients pick the receiving option that matches their threat model. Donors see a single QR; the wallet picks the right protocol.
block3.publicSummary: Works in every Bitcoin wallet. Fast and verifiable on-chain.
block3.privateSummary: BIP-352 silent payments. Donations land at unlinkable outputs.

all.title: Campaigns
all.description: Browse every campaign published on Agora.
all.sectionTagline: Featured campaigns first, then the rest of the network. Search or sort to refine.
all.heroBody: Every fundraiser published on Nostr, gathered into one place. Browse the full network, find a cause that matters to you, and back it directly with Bitcoin.
all.noMatchHint: Try a different search term, or clear the search to see every campaign.
all.allHiddenHint: Every campaign on the network has been hidden by moderators. Toggle “Show hidden” to view them.
all.emptyHint: No campaigns have been published yet. Be the first.

settings.loginRequired: You need to be signed in to manage organizers.

network.title: Network
network.subtitle: Manage data usage, relays, and file upload servers.
network.reduceDataUsageDesc: For metered or slow connections. Videos won't autoplay, background video previews are skipped, and images wait for a tap before loading.
network.useImageProxyDesc: Fetches smaller WebP versions of images instead of full-resolution originals.
network.proxyApiDesc: Must speak the <0>wsrv.nl / weserv</0> API. Self-hosters can point this at their own instance.
network.uploadQualityDesc: Compressed resizes large images and picks the smallest format. Original uploads images exactly as-is.

advanced.title: Advanced
advanced.subtitle: Wallet connections, system configuration, and other advanced options for power users.
advanced.intro: Wallet connections, system configuration, and other advanced options.
advanced.torToggleDesc: Connects to relays and loads media over the Tor network for stronger privacy. Expect slower performance and a short delay while Tor connects.
advanced.torApplyNote: Turning this on routes all traffic through Tor. While it connects, other content won't load (a banner shows the status) — nothing is sent outside Tor. Turning it off switches back to a direct connection.

language.title: Language
language.subtitle: Choose the language used throughout the app.
language.translationNote: Translations are a work in progress. Untranslated text falls back to English.

verifier.title: Verifier
verifier.subtitle: Become a verifier by describing how you vet campaigns.
verifier.prompt: Tell us what steps you take to verify campaigns. Include as much detail as possible so donors know they can trust your judgement.
verifier.placeholder: Describe how you verify campaigns — the checks you run, the evidence you require, how you confirm an organizer's identity, and anything else that helps donors trust your judgement.
verifier.withdrawConfirmBody: Your "How We Verify" statement will be removed from your profile. You can publish a new one anytime.
verifier.withdrawnToast: Your verifier statement has been withdrawn.
verifier.disclaimer: Anyone can become a verifier. Your statement is published publicly to Nostr and shown prominently on your profile. It does not grant any special permissions — it simply tells donors how you vet campaigns.
verifier.loginGateTitle: Sign in to become a verifier
verifier.loginGateBody: You need to be signed in to publish a verifier statement.

organizations.seoDescription: Onboard your organization onto {{appName}}, publish a public verification statement, and earn donor trust.

hero.body: Bring your organization onto {{appName}} and publish a public statement describing how you vet the campaigns you stand behind. No application, no gatekeepers — your reputation does the work.

steps.title: Onboard in three steps
steps.lede: {{appName}} is built on Nostr, so your organization brings its own identity. Getting set up takes minutes.

step1.title: Bring your profile
step1.body: Sign in with your organization's Nostr profile — or create one in seconds. Your identity is yours, portable across every Nostr app, and not controlled by {{appName}}.

step2.title: Describe your process
step2.body: Write a public statement explaining how you verify campaigns: the checks you run, the evidence you require, and how you confirm an organizer's identity.

step3.title: Publish & earn trust
step3.body: Your statement appears on your profile and signals to donors that you stand behind the campaigns you verify. Update or withdraw it any time.

getStarted.title: Become a verifier
getStarted.lede: Set up your organization and publish your verification statement in a few quick steps.

getStartedCard.title: Set up your organization
getStartedCard.body: We'll walk you through creating your organization's profile, publishing your verification statement, and verifying your first campaign.

organizations.loginGateTitle: Sign in with your organization's profile
organizations.loginGateBody: Log in with your organization's Nostr profile, or create one, to get started. Once you're signed in, you can publish your verification statement here.

tutorial.title: How to verify a campaign
tutorial.lede: Your statement is live. Here's how to vouch for a campaign you trust — it takes two taps from any campaign card.

open.title: Open the menu
open.body: Tap the three-dots button on any campaign card.

verify.title: Choose "Verify this campaign"
verify.body: Pick the verify action — shown only to verifiers.

confirm.title: Confirm & you're done
confirm.body: Attest it's authentic. Your badge joins the card.

organizers.appointDesc: Grant a user permission to pin posts on a specific country feed.
organizers.alreadyOrganizer: This user is already an organizer for that country
organizers.adminRequiredDesc: Only platform admins can appoint or remove organizers.

wallet.seoDescription: Hierarchical-deterministic Bitcoin wallet derived from your Nostr nsec.

loggedOut.title: Bitcoin Wallet
loggedOut.description: A hierarchical wallet derived from your Nostr identity. Fresh address per receive, full transaction history, no address reuse.
loggedOut.requiresNsec: Requires login with an nsec (your Nostr private key).

unsupported.title: Wallet unavailable
unsupported.extension: Your browser extension keeps your secret key isolated, so we can't derive child keys for your wallet.
unsupported.bunker: Your remote signer (NIP-46 bunker) keeps your secret key on the bunker side, so we can't derive child keys for your wallet.
unsupported.other: Your login type doesn't expose the secret key needed to derive your wallet.
unsupported.instructions: Log out and sign in again with your nsec to use the wallet.

receiveDialog.title: Receive Bitcoin
receiveDialog.description: Share an address to receive bitcoin.

tx.silentDetectedTitle: Detected via BIP-352 silent payment scan

walletLegacy.seoDescription: Recover funds from previous Agora wallet generations.
walletLegacy.title: Legacy wallet recovery
walletLegacy.subtitle: Pick the previous wallet you used and sweep its balance into your current wallet.
walletLegacy.loggedOut: Log in with your Nostr identity to look up funds in your old wallets.
walletLegacy.scanning: Scanning the V2 beta wallet for funds…

v2Detected.title: Funds found in the V2 Prelaunch Beta Wallet
v2Detected.body: We found <0>{{amount}}</0> at addresses from your old V2 beta wallet. Open the V2 sweep below to move them into your current wallet.

v2Beta.label: V2 Prelaunch Beta Wallet
v2Beta.description: Sweep funds from the nsec-as-seed BIP-86 wallet that shipped before BIP-39 derivation.

v1Breeze.label: V1 Breeze Wallet
v1Breeze.description: Sweep the on-chain balance from the legacy Lightning custody.

doubleTweak.label: Double-tweak SP Fix
doubleTweak.description: Recover silent payments stranded by an earlier sending bug and sweep them back into your wallet.

walletLegacy.footnote: Each option is a one-way sweep into your current wallet. The legacy wallet itself is not restored — only the funds it held are recovered.

walletRecovery.seoDescription: Recover funds from a previous Lightning wallet and transfer them to your Nostr-derived Bitcoin address.
walletRecovery.title: Recover Old Wallet
walletRecovery.heading: Recover your old wallet
walletRecovery.subheading: If you previously held funds in the Lightning wallet, you can sweep them to your Nostr-derived Bitcoin address. Your old wallet is not restored — this is a one-time, one-way transfer.

loggedOut.title: Log in to recover
loggedOut.description: Log in with the Nostr identity that owned the old Lightning wallet so the funds can be swept to its Bitcoin address.

phrase.title: Recovery phrase
phrase.description: Paste the 12-word recovery phrase from your old wallet.
phrase.checkingRelays: Checking your relays for an encrypted backup…
phrase.backupFoundDesc: We found an encrypted backup of your old wallet's recovery phrase published from this account.
phrase.warning: Only paste a recovery phrase you trust. Anyone with this phrase can spend the funds. After the sweep completes, delete it from any place you copied it from.
phrase.sweepButton: Sweep funds to my Bitcoin address

sweeping.dontClose: Don't close this tab. This can take up to a minute while the old wallet syncs and the transaction is broadcast.

success.title: Recovery complete
success.sent: Sent {{sats}} sats to your Bitcoin address.

errors.noNip44: Your signer does not support NIP-44 decryption, which is required to read the relay backup. Paste your 12-word recovery phrase manually instead.
errors.decryptFailed: Could not decrypt the relay backup. Paste your 12-word recovery phrase manually instead.
errors.invalidWordCount: Recovery phrase must be 12 or 24 words.
errors.noBalance: Your old wallet has no spendable balance. There is nothing to recover.

toast.backupLoadedDesc: Recovery phrase decrypted from your relays.

walletBackup.heading: Wallet seed phrase
walletBackup.dialogDescription: You can access your Agora wallet in any Bitcoin wallet by importing this 24-word backup.
walletBackup.hidden: Tap to display your 24-word seed phrase.
walletBackup.warning: Anyone with these words can spend your Bitcoin. Never share them, never type them into a website, never store them in plaintext outside a password manager.

walletMigrate.seoDescription: Sweep funds from your legacy Agora wallet (nsec-as-seed) into the new BIP-39 wallet.
walletMigrate.title: Migrate Old Wallet
walletMigrate.heading: Migrate to your new wallet
walletMigrate.subheading: Agora's Bitcoin wallet now uses a BIP-39 seed phrase so it imports into any wallet app. Funds at your old addresses are still safe — this page sweeps them into your new wallet in one transaction.
walletMigrate.warning: This builds a single sweep transaction that consumes every UTXO from your old wallet and sends the total (minus a small network fee) to your new wallet's first receive address. Once broadcast, the move is final and cannot be undone.

loggedOut.title: Log in to migrate
loggedOut.description: Log in with the Nostr identity that owns the old wallet so the funds can be swept to your new address.

unsupported.title: Wallet not available
unsupported.description: Migration only works for nsec logins, because the old wallet's keys were derived directly from your nsec. Browser-extension and remote-signer logins cannot run this migration.

noBlockbook.title: Blockbook not configured
noBlockbook.description: Migration needs a Blockbook indexer URL to scan your old addresses and broadcast the sweep transaction.

noFunds.title: Nothing to migrate
noFunds.description: Your old wallet has no spendable balance. Everything is already in your new wallet.

detected.title: Funds in your old wallet
detected.description: These funds will be moved to your new wallet in one transaction.

destination.title: Destination
destination.description: Your new wallet's first receive address.

progress.dontClose: Don't close this tab while the sweep is in progress.

success.title: Migration complete
success.sent: Sent {{sats}} sats to your new wallet ({{fee}} sats fee).

errors.noV1Account: Could not access the old wallet's keys.
errors.noDestination: Your new wallet has no receive address yet. Wait for the wallet page to load and try again.
errors.noBlockbook: Blockbook indexer is not configured. Migration cannot scan your old addresses.
errors.noFunds: No spendable funds found at the old addresses.

toast.successDesc: {{sats}} sats moved to your new wallet.

walletDoubleTweak.seoDescription: Recover silent payments stranded on-chain by an earlier sending bug and sweep them into your wallet.
walletDoubleTweak.title: Double-tweak SP Fix
walletDoubleTweak.heading: Recover stranded silent payments
walletDoubleTweak.subheading: An earlier version of Agora encoded some silent-payment outputs to the wrong key, so they never showed up in your wallet. This tool re-scans the chain for those outputs and sweeps any it finds into your wallet.
walletDoubleTweak.loggedOut: Log in with the Nostr identity that received the silent payments so they can be recovered.

unsupported.title: Wallet unavailable
unsupported.description: Recovery needs a wallet that can sign locally. Log in with your nsec to continue.

noIndexer.title: No silent-payment indexer configured
noIndexer.description: A BIP-352 indexer URL is required to scan for stranded payments. Set one in wallet settings and try again.

scan.title: Scan for stranded payments
scan.description: Choose the block height to start scanning from. Recovery checks every block from there to the chain tip.

noFunds.title: Nothing to recover
noFunds.description: No stranded silent payments were found in the scanned range. Try an earlier start height if you expected funds.

found.title: Stranded payments found
found.description: These outputs can be swept back into your wallet.

destination.title: Funds will be sent to
destination.description: A fresh receive address in your current wallet.

walletDoubleTweak.dontClose: Don't close this page until the recovery finishes.

errors.noDestination: No destination address available. Open your wallet first.
errors.noBlockbook: No Blockbook server configured. Set one in wallet settings.
errors.sweepGeneric: Could not complete the recovery sweep.

walletDoubleTweak.footnote: This is a one-way sweep into your current wallet. Only affected silent payments are touched; nothing else changes.

walletSend.title: Send Bitcoin

reuse.acknowledge: I understand the risk — send anyway
reuse.historyWarning: This address has already been used on-chain. Sending your private funds here links them to its existing transaction history and undoes the privacy of your silent payments. Use a silent payment address (sp1…) instead, or send anyway.
reuse.ownPublicWarning: This is an address from your own public wallet. Sending your private funds to it merges the two wallets and links them together. Use a silent payment address (sp1…) instead, or send anyway.

walletSend.ownSilentPaymentBlocked: This is your own silent payment address. You can't send from your public wallet to your private wallet — doing so co-mingles your private silent payment funds with coins already exposed on the public ledger, linking your private wallet to a known identity and destroying its privacy. Send to a different address instead.

scanError.title: Couldn't read that QR code

broadcastError.networkBody: Check your connection and try again.

key.heading: Your Key
key.extensionBody: You're signed in with a browser extension (NIP-07). Your secret key is stored there — manage or export it from the extension itself.
key.bunkerBody: You're signed in with a remote signer (NIP-46). Your secret key is held by that signer and cannot be exported from {{appName}}.
key.explainer: This secret key controls your account on {{appName}} and seeds your Bitcoin wallet. Anyone with it can post as you AND spend your Bitcoin. Store it in a password manager or somewhere else only you can access. If you want to import the Bitcoin wallet into another app, use the seed phrase below — it's safer to share, only controls the wallet, and can't be used to impersonate you.
key.warning: NEVER share your secret key with anyone. Avoid screenshotting it or pasting it anywhere except a password manager. If shared, others will be able to access your account.
key.savedToFile: Your secret key was saved to the Documents folder on your device.
key.saveFailedDescription: Could not save the key. Please copy it manually.
key.copyFailedDescription: Could not access the clipboard. Reveal the key and copy it manually.

what-is-ditto.question: What is {{appName}}?
answer: {{appName}} is a platform for sending Bitcoin donations — public or private — directly to recipients. There's no middleman, no payment processor, and no account that can be frozen.
{{appName}} is built on Nostr, so your identity isn't locked to this site — you own it.

cost-to-use.question: Does {{appName}} cost anything?
answer: **No.** {{appName}} takes no platform fee. When you donate, you pay only the Bitcoin network fee that goes to miners — not to us.

who-made-this.question: Who made {{appName}}?
answer: {{appName}} is built by [Soapbox](https://soapbox.pub), an open-source team building tools for the Nostr ecosystem, in collaboration with the [World Liberty Congress](https://worldlibertycongress.org/).

send-bitcoin-onchain.question: How does sending Bitcoin work?
answer: When a recipient creates a campaign, they choose what kinds of payments to accept: **public**, **private**, or **both**.
**Public** donations are real Bitcoin on-chain. They settle on the public blockchain, work in every Bitcoin wallet, and are visible to anyone.

**Private** donations use **silent payments** (BIP-352). They settle on-chain too, but the transaction can't be linked back to the recipient's donation code — so they stay out of public donor lists and totals. The donor needs a wallet that supports silent payments — we recommend [Ditto Wallet](https://ditto.pub) or [Dana](https://github.com/cygnet3/dana/releases/download/v0.7.4/app-live-release.apk).

When a campaign accepts **both**, {{appName}} shows a single QR code that encodes both endpoints. Silent-payment wallets read it as private; other wallets fall back to the public address. Donors don't have to choose — their wallet picks the right path automatically.
Either way, the payment goes straight to the recipient. {{appName}} never touches the funds.

connect-wallet.question: What is the wallet on {{appName}}?
answer: Your {{appName}} wallet is a **self-custodial** Bitcoin wallet, built from your Nostr key. {{appName}} never holds your funds — the wallet runs in your browser and signs transactions locally with a key only you control. It can receive Bitcoin both ways — as a public address that any Bitcoin wallet can pay, and as a silent-payments code that capable wallets can pay privately. There's nothing to sign up for; it exists the moment you have an account.

The wallet has two tabs: **Public** (your regular Bitcoin address) and **Private** (silent payments). The two balances are kept strictly separate and can never be spent together — that isolation is what keeps private donations unlinkable on-chain. When you create a campaign, you pick whether to accept public payments, private payments, or both. To spend what you receive, see the **Recipient Guide**.
You can also export your wallet's 24-word seed phrase and import it into any standard Bitcoin wallet — see "Can I export my {{appName}} wallet to another Bitcoin wallet?" below.

export-wallet.question: Can I export my {{appName}} wallet to another Bitcoin wallet?
answer: Yes. Your {{appName}} wallet is fully self-custodial. {{appName}} never holds your Bitcoin and never sees your keys — the wallet runs entirely in your browser, signing transactions locally with a key derived from your Nostr secret key (nsec).

Open the wallet from the menu, tap the three-dot menu in the top right, and choose **Back up wallet**. You'll see a 24-word BIP-39 seed phrase. Write it down somewhere safe (or save it in a password manager) — **anyone with these words can spend your Bitcoin.**

You can import those 24 words into any standard Bitcoin wallet: **Sparrow**, **Electrum**, **BlueWallet**, **Phoenix**, a **Trezor**, or a **Ledger**. {{appName}} uses standard BIP-86 (Taproot) and BIP-352 (silent payments) derivation paths, so no special configuration is needed.
After importing, the other wallet shows the same balance and history as your {{appName}} wallet — they're the same wallet, just two views of it.

Seed-phrase export is only available when you sign in with an nsec. Browser-extension and remote-signer (bunker) logins keep your secret key isolated, so {{appName}} can't derive the seed in those modes.

donations-are-public-general.question: Are donations on {{appName}} public?
answer: It depends on which kind of payment the recipient accepts.
**Public donations** are recorded on the Bitcoin blockchain and on Nostr. Anyone can see the amounts, timing, and addresses.
**Private donations** use silent payments. They're not publicly linkable to the recipient's donation code, don't appear in donor lists, and don't count toward public totals.

When a campaign accepts both, the donor's wallet decides which path to use — silent-payment-capable wallets pay privately, others pay the public address. Read the **Donor Guide** and **Recipient Guide** for the full picture.

why-donations-pending.question: Why do some donations say "pending"?
answer: A **pending** donation is real Bitcoin that's already been sent — it's just waiting to be confirmed by the network. The donor's wallet has broadcast the transaction, but it hasn't been included in a block yet.

Bitcoin produces a new block roughly every 10 minutes, and miners pick transactions based on the fee the donor paid. Most donations confirm within an hour; lower-fee ones can take longer when the network is busy. {{appName}} re-checks confirmation status automatically, so the **pending** label disappears on its own once a block lands.

The funds are on the way — the recipient will see the donation count toward the campaign total as soon as it confirms. There's nothing the recipient or {{appName}} can do to speed it up; only the donor's wallet can bump the fee.

censorship-resistance.question: What does "censorship-resistant" mean here?
answer: No company sits between a donor and a recipient. {{appName}} doesn't hold the funds and can't freeze the address.
As long as the Bitcoin network is running, donations can be sent and received. {{appName}} itself going offline wouldn't stop them.

why-onchain.question: Why Bitcoin?
answer: Bitcoin is the most widely supported and censorship-resistant payment rail in the world. Every Bitcoin wallet can send it.

On {{appName}}, recipients choose how to receive: **public** (a regular Bitcoin address) for maximum reach, **private** (silent payments) for unlinkable donations, or **both** so each donor's wallet picks the right path automatically. Donors who only have a consumer Bitcoin app can still contribute; donors with a silent-payments wallet get privacy by default.
The tradeoff is that public Bitcoin transactions are visible on the blockchain and pay a miner fee. The Donor and Recipient guides explain how to handle both.

why-not-silent-payments.question: Does {{appName}} support silent payments?
answer: Yes — on both sides. {{appName}}'s built-in Bitcoin wallet supports BIP-352, so when you send Bitcoin from your {{appName}} wallet you can pay any silent-payment address (`sp1…`) directly. And when a recipient creates a campaign, they can accept silent payments alongside, or instead of, public Bitcoin payments.
Silent-payment donations still settle on-chain, but the transaction can't be linked back to the recipient's donation code — so they don't appear in public donor lists or totals.

Sending a silent payment from outside {{appName}} requires a wallet that supports BIP-352. Most consumer apps don't yet, but [Ditto Wallet](https://ditto.pub) and [Dana](https://github.com/cygnet3/dana/releases/download/v0.7.4/app-live-release.apk) do.

When a campaign accepts both kinds of payment, {{appName}} encodes them in a single QR code. Silent-payment-capable wallets pay privately; everyone else pays the public address. No donor is shut out, and no recipient is forced to choose between reach and privacy.

why-not-lightning.question: Why doesn't {{appName}} use Lightning?
answer: Lightning requires a Lightning wallet. The easiest ones (Wallet of Satoshi, Strike, Breez) are **custodial** — a company holds the funds and can be shut down, geo-blocked, or pressured into freezing accounts. Non-custodial Lightning is technically demanding and unreliable for newcomers.

We want {{appName}} to work for someone whose only Bitcoin experience is a regular consumer app like Cash App, Coinbase, Strike, Venmo, or PayPal. On-chain Bitcoin works with every wallet on the planet.

why-not-rotating-addresses.question: Why doesn't {{appName}} generate a new address for every donation?
answer: Doing this would require {{appName}} to act as a money-exchanging middleman — taking custody of the Bitcoin first and then forwarding it on to the recipient.

That would make us a money transmitter, subject to the regulations that come with that, and a single point of failure: shut down {{appName}}'s server and you've shut down every donation flowing through it.

Instead, each user's donation address is derived from their Nostr secret key (nsec) — the same key you sign in with. Donors send directly to the recipient, {{appName}} never touches the funds, and the platform itself can't be turned off to censor anyone. Recipients who want per-donation privacy can accept silent payments, which give the same unlinkability without anyone holding the money in the middle.

why-not-other-crypto.question: Why not Monero or another cryptocurrency?
answer: Bitcoin is by far the most widely adopted cryptocurrency. That means it's the easiest for donors to buy and send, and the easiest for recipients to receive, hold, and spend.

Privacy-focused coins like Monero offer different privacy tradeoffs than Bitcoin, but they're unsupported by most consumer apps and harder to convert back to local currency. Asking either side of a donation to first acquire a niche cryptocurrency is a barrier {{appName}} won't put in the way. For Bitcoin donations themselves, silent payments cover the unlinkability use case without leaving the Bitcoin ecosystem.

what-is-nostr.question: What is Nostr?
answer: Nostr is an open network where **you** own your account, not a company. Your identity is a cryptographic key you control, not a username on someone else's server.

On {{appName}}, that same key is also what your donation address is derived from — your Nostr secret key (nsec) seeds your Bitcoin wallet, which is why you can receive Bitcoin without signing up with anyone.

why-login-different.question: Why is my sign-in so different and long?
answer: Instead of a username and password controlled by a company, Nostr uses a pair of cryptographic keys.
Your "public key" (starts with **npub**) is your username. Your "secret key" (starts with **nsec**) is your password. The long string is what makes it virtually impossible to guess.

lose-secret-key.question: What happens if I lose my secret key?
answer: **There is no "forgot password" button.** Nobody can reset it for you. If you lose it, your account — and any Bitcoin sitting at your donation address — is gone forever.
**Save your secret key somewhere safe right now.** For tips, read [Managing Your Nostr Keys](https://soapbox.pub/blog/managing-nostr-keys).

manage-secret-key.question: Can I save my secret key in my phone's password manager?
answer: Yes. You can save it in your device's password manager (iCloud Keychain, 1Password, Bitwarden, etc.). On iPhone, saving it in Passwords lets you use Face ID or Touch ID to log in.
For a full guide, see [Managing Your Nostr Keys](https://soapbox.pub/blog/managing-nostr-keys).

send-bitcoin-lightning.question: How does sending Bitcoin over Lightning work?
answer: If a recipient has a Lightning address on their profile, you can send to that. Lightning settles in seconds and fees are tiny.
Lightning sends don't use {{appName}}'s donation address — they go straight to whatever Lightning wallet the recipient set up themselves. {{appName}}'s own donation flow is on-chain only.

what-are-zaps.question: What are zaps?
answer: Zaps are small Lightning tips on Nostr, separate from {{appName}}'s on-chain donation flow.

fyp.question: How does the feed work?
answer: Your feed shows campaigns and posts from people you follow. There's no algorithm deciding what you see.

what-are-relays.question: What are relays?
answer: Relays are the servers that store and deliver Nostr events — posts, donation receipts, profile info. The defaults work out of the box; you can add or remove relays in Settings > Network.

what-are-blossom.question: What are Blossom servers?
answer: Blossom servers store media files (campaign images, profile pictures) when you upload them. You can manage which servers you use in Settings > Network.

report-content.question: How do I report harmful content?
answer: Tap the three-dot menu (**...**) on any post and select "Report." You can mute or block users from the same menu.

vs-mastodon-bluesky.question: How is Nostr different from Mastodon or Bluesky?
answer: On Mastodon, your account lives on a specific server. On Bluesky, most accounts depend on one company. On Nostr, your identity is a key you control, and your donation address goes with you to any Nostr app.

profile-fields.question: What are profile fields?
answer: Profile fields let you add extra info to your profile — links, wallet addresses, music, photos, videos.

whatYouSee.label: What you see
whatYouSee.public: A regular Bitcoin address you can pay from anywhere.
whatYouSee.silent: The same QR. Your wallet picks the silent-payment endpoint if it supports BIP-352.

walletSupport.label: Wallet support
walletSupport.public: Every Bitcoin wallet. Cash App, Coinbase, Strike, hardware, anything.
walletSupport.silent: Few wallets today. Most fall back to a regular Bitcoin transaction.

privacy.label: Privacy of the donation
privacy.public: Public on-chain. Your sending address is permanently linked to the campaign.
privacy.silent: Receiving side is unlinkable on-chain. Your sending wallet's trail is still public.

settlement.label: Settlement
settlement.silent: Same on-chain confirmations, but the recipient has to scan their wallet to find it.

whatDonorsSee.label: What donors see
whatDonorsSee.public: A regular Bitcoin address. Works with every wallet on earth.
whatDonorsSee.silent: A BIP-352 endpoint. Donors' wallets need silent-payments support; otherwise the donation falls back to a regular Bitcoin transaction.

receivingSpeed.label: Receiving speed
receivingSpeed.public: Push-style. Donations show up immediately on the campaign page.
receivingSpeed.silent: Manual scanning. Your wallet has to walk the blockchain looking for them. Minutes to hours, depending on the wallet.

pushNotifications.label: Push notifications
pushNotifications.public: Yes. You see new donations the moment they arrive.
pushNotifications.silent: No. Open the wallet and trigger a scan to discover them.

donorList.label: Donor list / campaign totals
donorList.public: Public forever. Amounts and sending addresses are visible to anyone.
donorList.silent: Private. The campaign page can't show silent-payments donation counts or totals.

ecosystem.label: Ecosystem maturity
ecosystem.silent: Bleeding-edge. Wallets are still buggy; expect missed payments that show up on a later scan.

bestFor.label: Best for
bestFor.public: Above-ground fundraisers where social proof and visibility help.
bestFor.silent: Campaigns where donor or recipient privacy matters more than the visible total.

watchOutFor.label: Watch out for
watchOutFor.public: Permanent public record of every donor.
watchOutFor.silent: Bumpy UX today. Some donations won't show until the recipient scans.

tldr.lede: Pay the Bitcoin address on the campaign page from any wallet you already have. If the campaign accepts silent payments and your wallet supports them, your donation is private automatically.

openCampaign.title: Open the campaign
openCampaign.body: You see a single QR code. If the campaign accepts both options, it encodes both endpoints; your wallet picks the right one.

payFromAnyWallet.title: Pay it from any wallet
payFromAnyWallet.body: Cash App, Coinbase, Strike, a hardware wallet, anything. Pay the amount plus the network fee.

arrivesDirectly.title: It arrives directly
arrivesDirectly.body: Funds settle straight to the recipient. {{appName}} doesn't hold or route them, and the address is derived from the recipient's Nostr secret key.

comparison.footnote: Campaigns can accept Public only, Silent only, or both. If both, the QR code carries both endpoints. Your wallet picks the one it can use.

publicVisible.title: Public donations are visible on-chain forever
publicVisible.body: A **Public** donation lands at a regular Bitcoin address tied to the campaign. Anyone can look up the address and see the amount, the time, and your sending address. **Silent** donations settle on-chain too, but the receiving side is unlinkable to the campaign so they stay out of public donor lists and totals.

privacy.heading: Donating privately
privacy.intro: These steps matter most for **Public** donations, where every transaction is permanently tied to a single address. **Silent** donations already hide the receiving side, so the risk is lower. Targeted analysis of your sending wallet is still possible either way, so if your risk is high these steps are worth taking. Pick one, or stack them.

silentWallet.name: Use a silent-payments wallet
silentWallet.purpose: Pay with a Bitcoin wallet that supports BIP-352. If the campaign accepts silent payments, your wallet uses that endpoint automatically.

nonKyc.name: Buy non-KYC Bitcoin
nonKyc.purpose: Buy Bitcoin peer-to-peer so it isn't linked to your government ID in the first place. Strongest privacy starting point.

coinjoin.name: Coinjoin first
coinjoin.purpose: Mix your Bitcoin with other people's coins so the output can't be traced to your KYC purchase. Useful when the campaign only accepts public.

freshWallet.name: Use a fresh wallet
freshWallet.purpose: Donate from a wallet that has never touched your main identity or a KYC exchange.

consumerApps.title: Consumer apps can't make you anonymous
consumerApps.body: Cash App, Coinbase, Strike, Venmo, Kraken, Binance, and PayPal all verify your ID. No matter how you send the donation, every transaction stays tied to your real identity. Use a non-custodial wallet you control.

silentToday.heading: A note on silent payments today
paragraphs: Silent payments are the most private way to receive Bitcoin on-chain, but the ecosystem is young. Most popular wallets can't send to a silent-payment endpoint yet, so when a wallet can't, the donation falls back to a regular Bitcoin transaction to the campaign's public address (if the campaign accepts both).

For recipients, silent-payment donations also arrive without push notifications and only appear after the recipient scans their wallet, which can take minutes to hours. None of this affects the safety of your funds; it just shapes the experience.

tldr.lede: Pick what to accept when you create your campaign: Public, Silent, or both. Either option is non-custodial. {{appName}} never holds your funds.
nextActions: Plan how you will cash out

howReceiving.heading: How receiving works
paragraphs: Your {{appName}} donation addresses are derived from your Nostr secret key (nsec) — the same key you sign in with seeds your Bitcoin wallet. When you create a campaign, you pick what to accept:
**Public payments only.** A regular Bitcoin address. Visible to everyone, works with every wallet.

**Silent payments only.** BIP-352 silent payments. The receiving side is unlinkable on-chain, so donations stay out of public donor lists and totals. Donors need a silent-payments-capable wallet to send. If they don't have one, their donation can't go through.
**Both.** {{appName}} generates a single QR code that encodes both endpoints. Silent-payments wallets read it as private; ordinary wallets pay the public address. Donors don't have to choose.
Accepting both is usually the right call: you get private donations from supporters who use a silent-payments wallet, and you stay open to donors whose only Bitcoin is in a consumer app.

Inside your {{appName}} wallet, these arrive as **two separate balances** — a **Public** tab (your regular Bitcoin address) and a **Private** tab (silent payments) — shown on the wallet's Public / Private tabs. {{appName}} keeps them strictly apart on purpose: the two balances can never be spent together. Mixing private funds with public ones in a single transaction would link your unlinkable silent-payment donations back to your public address on-chain and destroy the privacy they exist to provide.

whatEveryoneSees.heading: What everyone can see
paragraphs: If your campaign accepts public payments, anyone considering supporting you can look up the address and see the public donation history.

Silent-payment donations aren't part of that record. They're invisible to outside observers and don't show in the campaign's public totals; new donors only see whatever you publish about the campaign's progress.

comparison.footnote: You can't switch a campaign's accepted payment options after it's created. If you change your mind, make a new campaign.

silentToday.heading: A note on silent payments today
paragraphs: Silent payments are the most private way to receive Bitcoin on-chain, but the ecosystem is young. Most popular wallets can't send to a silent-payment endpoint yet, so when a donor's wallet can't, the donation falls back to a regular Bitcoin transaction to your public address (if you accept both).

Silent-payment donations also arrive without push notifications and only appear after you scan your wallet, which can take minutes to hours. None of this affects the safety of your funds; it just shapes the day-to-day experience.

twoWallets.title: Keep your Public and Private balances separate
twoWallets.body: Your {{appName}} wallet shows two tabs: **Public** (your regular Bitcoin address) and **Private** (silent payments). They're kept strictly apart, and the two balances can never be spent in the same transaction — that isolation is what keeps your private donations unlinkable. When you cash out from the **Private** tab, send to a silent-payment address (**sp1…**), which stays private. If you instead send private funds to a bare on-chain address, {{appName}} checks it: if the address has been used before, or belongs to your own public wallet, it warns you and makes you tap **Send anyway**, because doing so links your private funds to that address's public history. Sending to an **sp1…** address never triggers the warning.

sweep.title: Sweep to a wallet you control
sweep.body: Your {{appName}} wallet is already self-custodial — you, and only you, control the keys. For a desktop or hardware-wallet view of the same balance, open the wallet menu, choose **Back up wallet**, and import the 24-word seed into [Sparrow](https://sparrowwallet.com), [BlueWallet](https://bluewallet.io), or a hardware wallet (Trezor, Ledger). If you'd rather move the funds to a different key entirely — for example, to a Lightning wallet like [Phoenix](https://phoenix.acinq.co) — send a regular Bitcoin transaction from your {{appName}} wallet.

dontSit.title: Don't sit on funds in the browser
dontSit.body: Especially for high-value campaigns, store the wallet's seed phrase offline (paper, metal backup, or a password manager on a dedicated device) and consider a hardware wallet. The seed you back up from {{appName}} is the same wallet — secure the words and you've got cold storage.

cashout.heading: Cashing out privately
cashout.intro: Spending on-chain creates a trail unless you break it first. Cash out from the **Private** tab to keep silent-payment donations unlinkable — send to a silent-payments wallet first (an **sp1…** address), then spend onward; the hop breaks the link between your campaign and what comes next. Don't send private funds to a reused or public address, which re-links them. The other options below also work and have their own trade-offs.

silentHop.name: Silent-payments wallet hop
silentHop.purpose: Move your donations to a silent-payments wallet ([Ditto Wallet](https://ditto.pub), [Dana](https://github.com/cygnet3/dana/releases/download/v0.7.4/app-live-release.apk)). From there your downstream spending isn't tied to the campaign.

lightningSwap.name: Lightning swap
lightningSwap.purpose: Atomic-swap on-chain Bitcoin to Lightning. Lightning payments don't hit the public blockchain.

coinjoin.name: Coinjoin
coinjoin.purpose: Mix your Bitcoin with other users' coins so the output can't be linked back to the input.

peerToPeer.name: Peer-to-peer
peerToPeer.purpose: Trade Bitcoin for fiat directly with another person or through a broker on Bisq, HodlHodl, or RoboSats.

spendDirectly.name: Spend it directly
spendDirectly.purpose: Buy gift cards (Amazon, Uber, groceries, travel) straight from Bitcoin without converting to cash first.

tumblers.title: Avoid centralized tumblers
tumblers.body: Custodial mixers can steal your coins, log who sent what, or turn out to be law-enforcement honeypots. Use a silent-payments hop or a non-custodial coinjoin instead.
